title: 7_Mashaw_Due_Process_Calculus_1976
description: Jerry L. Mashaw — The Supreme Court's Due Process Calculus in Mathews v. Eldridge (44 U. Chi. L. Rev. 28 (1976))
show_notes:
Jerry L. Mashaw — The Supreme Court's Due Process Calculus in Mathews v. Eldridge (44 U. Chi. L. Rev. 28 (1976))

Jerry Mashaw dissects the Supreme Court's three-factor due process test from Mathews v. Eldridge — weighing the private interest, the risk of error, and the government's administrative burden. He argues that this utilitarian calculus, for all its apparent rigor, rests on an impoverished theory of procedural value that slights the dignitary and participatory interests procedure also serves. A foundational critique of how American law decides how much process is due.

Chapters:
36:01 I. The Supreme Court's analysis in Eldridge is not informed by systematic attention
